title: Macaroni Cheese

overview.description: Baked macaroni pasta coated in a rich, creamy cheese sauce and finished with a golden, crisp topping.

ingredients: 1 tsp English mustard or Dijon mustard
method: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Bring a large saucepan of salted water to the boil.
Cook the macaroni for 2 minutes less than the packet instructions suggest.
Drain thoroughly.
Melt the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the flour and stir continuously for 1–2 minutes.
Gradually whisk in the milk until smooth.
Add the cream.
Bring the sauce gently to a simmer, stirring regularly, until thickened.
Stir in the mustard and smoked paprika if using.
Remove the sauce from the heat.
Add the cheddar, mozzarella and Parmesan gradually, stirring until melted and smooth.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Add the drained macaroni to the cheese sauce.
Stir until thoroughly coated.
Transfer to a large ovenproof dish.
Mix the breadcrumbs and grated cheddar for the topping.
Scatter evenly over the macaroni.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until bubbling and golden.
Allow to rest for 5 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: The classic cheese sauce begins with a roux, which thickens the milk and allows the cheese to form a smooth sauce rather than simply melting into a thin liquid.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a smooth cheese sauce and how to slightly undercook pasta so it finishes cooking without becoming soft and mushy in the oven.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A comforting family pasta dish that is inexpensive, freezer-friendly and adaptable enough to serve as either a main meal or a side.
chapman-tips: Undercook the macaroni slightly because it will continue cooking in the oven.
Remove the sauce from the heat before adding the cheese to reduce the chance of it becoming grainy.
Use a combination of cheeses for better flavour and texture.
Mature cheddar provides the main flavour.
Mozzarella helps with stretch and texture.
Mustard makes the cheese flavour taste stronger without making the dish taste strongly of mustard.
Don't make the sauce too thick before adding the pasta.
For a particularly crisp top, add the breadcrumbs just before baking.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well. Freeze in individual portions or as a complete assembled dish before baking.
notes.leftovers: Reheats well, although adding a splash of milk before reheating helps restore creaminess.
